title: IT WAS JUST AN ACCIDENT
logline: After a fatal car accident, a man and his companions struggle to dispose of the victim's body while caring for the deceased's pregnant wife and daughter, leading to a moral reckoning about responsibility and human decency.
antagonistOrConflict: Moral guilt and the practical consequences of covering up a fatal accident

actBreaks.description: The group commits to helping dispose of Eghbal's body, crossing the moral threshold from witnesses to accomplices.

validation.ruling: Scene #11 fundamentally changes the story direction when Shiva sees the kidnapped Eghbal in the chest and agrees to help identify him, transforming her from potential victim to active participant in the kidnapping plot.

actBreaks.description: The pregnant woman goes into labor, forcing them to take her to the hospital and dramatically escalating their exposure and moral complexity.

validation.ruling: Scene #25 represents a major shift where Vahid poses as Niloufar's uncle at the hospital, fully committing to helping his victim's family while still holding the father captive - this ironic reversal fundamentally changes the story's moral landscape.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This is a tightly wound moral thriller that operates like a pressure cooker, steadily building tension through a series of increasingly compromising situations. The structure follows a classical three-act progression but with an Iranian neorealist sensibility that grounds the escalating stakes in mundane, realistic details. The script's genius lies in how it traps its characters (and audience) in a web of complicity that begins with a simple accident and evolves into a complex moral maze where every decision leads to deeper entanglement.

The pacing is methodical and deliberate, reflecting the characters' careful navigation of their predicament, but punctuated by moments of crisis that force rapid decisions and reveal character. The desert scenes serve as both literal and metaphorical spaces for burial and confession, while the urban scenes create a claustrophobic sense of being watched and judged by society.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains steady tension through its middle section (scenes 7-21) with a series of logistical challenges that feel increasingly urgent. The pace accelerates dramatically when the pregnant woman goes into labor (scene 25), creating a medical emergency that forces the characters into public spaces and official institutions. The final act slows to allow for moral reckoning and character revelation, particularly in the extended dialogue scenes in the van (scenes 31-32).

incitingIncident.description: Vahid digs a hole in the desert, drags the conscious and bound man to it, and begins burying him alive while the man pleads about his pregnant wife and children.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. DESERT OUTSIDE THE CITY - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is ordinary world setup, not the inciting incident. Scene #4 is the true inciting incident where Vahid buries a man alive in the desert while the victim pleads about his pregnant wife and children - this irreversibly shatters the status quo and establishes the main conflict that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: The group waits by an empty grave in the desert while debating whether to give Eghbal a chance to understand why he was captured, with Hamid arguing for immediate execution.

validation.ruling: Scene #22 occurs at the perfect midpoint timing (57.8%) where the group debates Eghbal's fate in the desert, with Hamid arguing for execution while others want to give him a chance to understand why he was captured - this represents the point of no return where they must decide whether to become killers or show mercy.

climax.description: Eghbal awakens and attacks Vahid when pulled from the chest, overpowering him until Shiva strikes Eghbal's head with a shovel, knocking him unconscious.
climax.sceneHeading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS OUT OF THE CITY - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #31 is the climactic confrontation where Eghbal escapes and physically fights Vahid, overpowering him until Shiva strikes Eghbal with a shovel - this is the peak dramatic tension and ultimate confrontation that resolves the main story question of whether they will kill or spare their captive.

scenes.heading: I/E. CAR ON COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A family drives on a country road at night with their daughter Niloufar singing loudly to music while her parents reluctantly join in, the father nervously watching passing cars.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUT SIDE THE HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid wakes up in his van outside a house, feeling pain in his side and searching for pills while his phone rings.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid follows a man in a taxi, then deliberately accelerates his van and hits the man who falls to the ground before quickly loading him into the van.
scenes.turningPointNote: Vahid commits the central violent act that drives the entire story

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT OUTSIDE THE CITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid digs a hole in the desert, drags the conscious and bound man to it, and begins burying him alive while the man pleads about his pregnant wife and children.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY OUTSKIRTS - DAY
scenes.summary: The van travels from the desert back toward the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid parks the van on a busy street and walks to a bookstore across the street.

scenes.heading: INT. BOOKSTORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid meets his friend Salar in the bookstore, mentions digging a hole in the desert, and asks for urgent help while Salar urges him to see a doctor.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE THE BOOKSTORE - DAY
scenes.summary: Salar gives Vahid a piece of paper with a woman named Shiva's phone number, then walks away without saying goodbye.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid drives through the city repeatedly calling Shiva's number with no answer until his phone finally rings and he brakes sharply to answer it.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - RESIDENTIAL DISTRICT - DAY
scenes.summary: Shiva photographs a wedding while taking a phone call, then meets Vahid who asks if she's the Mrs. Biglari who was arrested.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - RESIDENTIAL DISTRICT - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid shows Shiva the bound and blindfolded man (Eghbal) in his van's chest, revealing a plastic leg, while Shiva becomes petrified and they discuss what to do next.
scenes.turningPointNote: Shiva becomes complicit in Vahid's crime, expanding the conspiracy

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS DAY
scenes.summary: A wedding couple sits in the back of the van while Shiva and Vahid sit in front, with the couple being told they mustn't be heard.

scenes.heading: INT. PHARMACY - DAY
scenes.summary: Shiva talks to a doctor at a pharmacy counter while Vahid waits, and the doctor retrieves medication from a back room.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET OUTSIDE PHARMACY - DAY
scenes.summary: Shiva gives Vahid earplugs and medication to sedate the captive man, while Vahid worries the drugs might kill him immediately.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Shiva points out a man named Hamid to Vahid, then approaches him across the street where they argue and he pushes her to the ground.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARPARK - DAY
scenes.summary: In a parking garage, Hamid examines the unconscious Eghbal's legs and scars, then suddenly attacks him violently while cursing before the others restrain him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hamid's violent recognition of Eghbal confirms the victim's identity and escalates the group's commitment to revenge

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - BUSY STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid tries to dismiss the group and handle the situation alone, but Hamid refuses to leave until he sees Eghbal's corpse while Ali decides to go.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: The group drives through the city with Hamid questioning Shiva about quitting smoking and making cryptic comments about their situation.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid, Hamid, Golrokh and Ali push the broken-down van through the streets while passersby mock them.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: At a gas station, Golrokh suddenly leans out of the van to vomit while yellow liquid trickles across the van floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUIET SPOT UNDER A BRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: The group cleans the van under a bridge while Hamid questions Shiva about Vahid, expressing suspicion that he might be working with their enemies.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - DUSK
scenes.summary: The group waits by an empty grave in the desert while debating whether to give Eghbal a chance to understand why he was captured, with Hamid arguing for immediate execution.
scenes.turningPointNote: The group's moral debate over giving their victim a final confrontation reveals their conflicting approaches to justice

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE EGHBAL’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The van slowly passes Eghbal's house at night while the group looks for signs of a trap, then parks nearby as a child's voice pleads over the phone for her daddy to come home because mommy won't wake up.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of Eghbal's family crisis forces the group to confront the human cost of their actions

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group drives to the hospital with Eghbal's unconscious pregnant wife lying on the chest where he's hidden, while his daughter Niloufar cries in Golrokh's arms.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vahid poses as Niloufar's uncle at the hospital reception while trying to get her pregnant mother admitted, but the receptionist insists her father must sign the consent forms.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - WAITING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vahid and Niloufar wait in the hospital while she eats, and she explains that her father forbids her from calling other family members for help.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET NEAR THE HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vahid tells the group that the baby was born and asks them to withdraw money from an ATM to help pay hospital expenses.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEAR THE ATM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group gathers around an ATM to withdraw money for Vahid, who then suggests they leave while he stays at the hospital longer.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid enters the maternity unit and finds Niloufar asleep on a chair.

scenes.heading: EXT. STR EET NEAR THE HOSPITAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vahid returns to find Shiva waiting by the van, and she explains she stayed because of a promise she made to Salar.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEHRAN - STREETS OUT OF THE CITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Eghbal awakens and attacks Vahid when pulled from the chest, overpowering him until Shiva strikes Eghbal's head with a shovel, knocking him unconscious.
scenes.turningPointNote: Shiva's violent intervention to save Vahid marks her full transformation from reluctant participant to active accomplice

scenes.heading: I/E. STREETS AND VAHID’S MOTHER’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vahid loads baby items into his van outside his mother's house with Omid's help, then freezes when he hears brakes and shouts, followed by the approaching sound of Eghbal's artificial limb.
scenes.turningPointNote: Eghbal's arrival at Vahid's family home represents the complete collapse of Vahid's attempt to escape consequences
